slides.title: Business overview

perimeter.label: Transaction perimeter
perimeter.subLabel: Entities under FDD scope of work

overviewBody: Revenue growth was supported by sustained transaction volume gains across mobile and gateway channels, supported by retention in long-standing issuer contracts.
Mix shifted toward higher-value service packages, expanding contribution margin despite onboarding costs in newly migrated client programs.
Direct processing costs increased with transaction growth, though supplier renegotiations partially offset infrastructure inflation.
Compensation costs increased due to targeted hiring in implementation and compliance teams, aligned with program complexity and release cadence.
Card manufacturing and managed ATM service lines introduced additional gross profit contribution while increasing operational complexity and service-level tracking needs.
Working-capital demands increased from settlement timing differences, requiring tighter daily treasury controls and more disciplined receivables collection routines.
Management launched a commercial-pricing refresh to protect margin on lower-scale customers and align SLA commitments with delivered support levels.
The organization expanded fraud-monitoring capabilities and integrated rule-based controls into customer onboarding, reducing observed loss events in pilot cohorts.
Digital enablement projects continue to improve operational resilience, but full value realization depends on completion of platform migration milestones.
Leadership priorities now focus on balancing profitable growth, service reliability, and disciplined cost governance through the next reporting period.

chart.source: Source: Management accounts and diligence working papers

slides.source: Source: Management accounts and diligence working papers.
